chunks.text: A correspondent sending reports from a war zone is a vital means to inform us of what is happening in the world.
Journalists who go to conflict areas stake their own lives to convey the truth.
They are prepared for every exposure to danger.
Via satellite phones and the internet
their images and words are transported across the world instantly.
From our living room, we witness a distant battlefield in a visible form.
But the truth of war is not only what is shown on camera.
Much pain is invisible and cannot be conveyed on screen.
The sadness of refugees who have lost their home, the suffering of children without intervention.
Among journalists, some sadly lose their lives on the ground.
In the war in Ukraine since 2022 alone, many reporters have been killed.
They have become victims of murder, accident, and assault.
Even so, they continue their reporting.
Because they carry the conviction that " if the truth is not told to the world
the same tragedy will happen again.
To protect the freedom of the press is to protect democracy.
And to the journalists who continue to carry the truth at risk
we cannot help feeling we owe something.
